category_title: Oppositional and boisterous students with disruptive behaviors
scenario_title: Independent writing task avoidance and distraction
minimal_context: Edward is expected to work on an independent writing task at his desk during the writing workshop.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Edward, it is time for independent writing. Please begin writing on your notepad.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Breaks pencil tip) I need to sharpen my pencil first.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, sharpen it and then get to work.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Writes one word and stands up) I have a question.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please sit back down and keep trying to write.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Breaks pencil again and whispers conspiratorially) You should warn teachers to keep an eye on your students.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Edward, it is time for independent writing. Please begin writing on your notepad.

STUDENT: (Breaks pencil tip) I need to sharpen my pencil first.

TUTOR: Okay, sharpen it and then get to work.

STUDENT: (Writes one word and stands up) I have a question.

TUTOR: Please sit back down and keep trying to write.

STUDENT: (Breaks pencil again and whispers conspiratorially) You should warn teachers to keep an eye on your students.
